---
domain: article-data
related: [routing-ssr, markdown-pipeline, admin-grid, build-tooling]
---

# Article Data Flow — Mental Model

## Article model (libs/shared/src/lib/models/article.model.ts)

- OWNS: `Article` interface — id, title, slug, excerpt, content (markdown), coverImage, tags, status, readTime, author, createdAt, updatedAt, publishedAt
- OWNS: `ArticleStatus` union — `'draft' | 'published' | 'archived'`
- OWNS: `ArticleIndex = Omit<Article, 'content'>` — feed-page payload
- INVARIANT: `slug` is the URL key; must be unique across published articles (used by prerender + sitemap + `getArticleBySlug`)
- INVARIANT: `id` format is `art-<timestamp>` — assigned server-side in POST `/api/articles`

## data/articles.json (source of truth)

- OWNS: full article corpus including draft/archived
- WRITES TO: published as `/data/articles.json` static asset (see `apps/blog/project.json` assets glob)
- READS FROM: hand-edited or written by admin API
- INVARIANT: shipped to clients as-is — drafts are filtered client-side, NOT removed from the file
- TENSION: full corpus (including drafts) is publicly fetchable from `/data/articles.json`; relies on draft content being non-sensitive

## data/articles-index.json (build artifact)

- OWNS: lightweight metadata-only mirror (no `content` field)
- WRITES TO: regenerated by `tools/generate-article-index.mjs` (prebuild) and by admin `writeArticles()`
- INVARIANT: must always be a strict `Omit<Article, 'content'>` projection of `articles.json`
- DECIDED: separate index file exists so the feed page doesn't download every article body — feed uses index, article page fetches full file

## ArticleService (libs/shared/src/lib/services/article.service.ts)

- OWNS: HTTP boundary for both public and admin reads
- READS FROM: `/data/articles-index.json` (`getPublishedIndex`), `/data/articles.json` (`getPublishedArticles`, `getArticleBySlug`), `/api/articles` (`getAllArticles` — admin only)
- WRITES TO: `/api/articles` (POST/PUT/DELETE), `/api/upload-image`, `/api/git/commit-and-push`
- INVARIANT: every public read filters via `isVisible(status)` — `status === 'published'` always passes; non-published statuses pass only when `isDevMode()` is true
- INVARIANT: `getArticleBySlug` re-fetches the full corpus per call — no client cache (relies on browser HTTP cache)
- TENSION: image upload uses `FileReader` → base64 → JSON POST; large images blow up payload (capped by Express `10mb` limit)
- DECIDED: drafts are visible on localhost (`isDevMode() === true`) so authors can preview unpublished articles before `commit-and-push` makes them live; production builds (GitHub Pages) hide drafts. Prerender step in `app.routes.server.ts` still filters to `status === 'published'`, so drafts never get a static page in the deployed site even though they remain in `articles.json`.

## api.routes.ts (Express, dev/prod-server only)

- OWNS: `/api/articles` CRUD, `/api/upload-image`, `/api/git/commit-and-push`
- WRITES TO: `data/articles.json`, `data/articles-index.json`, `apps/blog/public/images/articles/<file>`, git index/working tree
- INVARIANT: `writeArticles()` always rewrites BOTH `articles.json` and `articles-index.json` (index stays in sync)
- INVARIANT: `writeArticles()` calls `cleanupOrphanedImages()` — any file in `images/articles/` not referenced by any article's `coverImage` or `content` is deleted
- INVARIANT: image upload sanitizes filename (`[^a-zA-Z0-9._-] → -`, lowercased), prefixes `Date.now()`, validates path stays under `imageDir` (path-traversal guard)
- INVARIANT: only extensions `.jpg .jpeg .png .gif .webp .avif` accepted
- FLOW[save]: admin form → `ArticleService.saveArticle` → PUT `/api/articles/:id` → `writeArticles` → rewrite both JSONs → cleanup orphans
- FLOW[publish-flow]: admin saves → `git/commit-and-push` triggers `git add data/ apps/blog/public/images/articles/` → commit → push → CI rebuilds → static site deploys
- TENSION: orphan cleanup runs on EVERY write — no soft delete; if an image was added but article save fails halfway, it'll vanish on next save
- DECIDED: server-side `id` assignment (`art-${Date.now()}`) and timestamp stamping prevent client-set IDs from colliding
